entries.mechanism: PPAR-gamma controls adipocyte differentiation, lipid storage, and insulin-sensitizing transcription.

sideEffects.mechanism: Metabolic context and adiposity signal increase sensitivity to concentration spikes
PPARG biology links to lipid handling and adipocyte insulin sensitivity

prescribingActions.action: Use tolerability-first titration; avoid rapid weekly peak
prescribingActions.rationale: PPARG + concordant BMI/liver markers support slower escalation

prescribingActions.action: Track waist circumference, lipids, and ALT/AST before week-five escalation

planTriggers.detail: Split dose Day 1 + Day 4 to reduce peak exposure

entries.mechanism: Variant affects GLP-1 receptor trafficking/signaling, changing incretin-induced cellular response.
entries.source: PMID: 35723666 - Association of GLP1R Polymorphisms With the Incretin Response

sideEffects.mechanism: Receptor-level variability may increase symptom sensitivity during peak exposure
Altered GLP-1 signaling can change gastric tolerance

prescribingActions.action: Avoid manufacturer-default weekly peak in weeks 1-3
prescribingActions.rationale: GLP1R variant increases early intolerance risk at target receptor
Smooth plasma exposure reduces concentration spikes

planTriggers.detail: Retatrutide (GLP-1/GIP/glucagon triple agonist) preferred for receptor coverage

entries.mechanism: GIPR modulates glucose-dependent insulinotropic signaling and adipose nutrient handling.
entries.source: Genes 2022;13:1534 - GIPR SNP variants and metabolic health

sideEffects.mechanism: GIP pathway variability may shift post-meal insulinotropic and GI response

prescribingActions.rationale: GIPR signal supports pathway coverage for visceral-fat and insulin context

planTriggers.detail: Retatrutide selected for GIP + GLP-1 + glucagon pathway coverage

entries.mechanism: TCF7L2 risk alleles impair beta-cell sensitivity to GLP-1/GIP.
entries.source: PMID: 19934000 - TCF7L2 rs7903146 modulates incretin action

sideEffects.mechanism: Reduced incretin effect plus peak exposure increases early intolerance risk

prescribingActions.action: Use adaptive split cadence; physician checkpoint before week-five escalation
prescribingActions.rationale: TCF7L2 makes dose response less predictable

prescribingActions.action: Trend fasting and post-prandial glucose before each escalation
prescribingActions.rationale: Beta-cell incretin sensitivity is reduced in carriers

planTriggers.detail: Physician checkpoint before week 5; trend glucose and GI tolerance weekly

entries.mechanism: FTO-region variation is associated with appetite regulation and energy-intake behavior.
entries.source: PMID: 17434869 - FTO variant associated with body mass and obesity risk

sideEffects.mechanism: FTO-associated appetite regulation may blunt perceived satiety benefit

prescribingActions.action: Add protein preload and appetite-score instrumentation
prescribingActions.rationale: FTO homozygous state supports behavioral monitoring

planTriggers.detail: Track hunger, craving, and protein intake weekly

entries.mechanism: MC4R pathway regulates hypothalamic satiety signaling and energy homeostasis.
entries.source: PMID: 18454148 - Common variants near MC4R associated with fat mass and obesity

sideEffects.mechanism: Melanocortin satiety pathway may counteract early pharmacologic satiety

entries.mechanism: KCNJ11 encodes Kir6.2, a pancreatic beta-cell potassium-channel subunit.
entries.source: PMID: 12475775 - Pancreatic ATP-sensitive potassium channels and type 2 diabetes

sideEffects.mechanism: Beta-cell channel biology may blunt predictable insulin secretion response

prescribingActions.action: Trend glucose response before dose acceleration

planTriggers.detail: Trend fasting and post-prandial glucose before each escalation

entries.mechanism: PNPLA3 variation is associated with hepatic triglyceride remodeling and fatty-liver susceptibility.
entries.source: PMID: 18820647 - PNPLA3 variant associated with hepatic fat content

sideEffects.mechanism: Hepatic fat susceptibility plus borderline baseline enzymes

prescribingActions.rationale: PNPLA3 + borderline enzymes raise hepatic surveillance priority

prescribingActions.action: Hold escalation if ALT/AST rise >2x baseline
prescribingActions.rationale: Hepatic safety context from genotype and biomarkers

planTriggers.detail: Hold if ALT/AST >2x baseline, severe GI intolerance, or eGFR decline
